Untitled Project
GHOST Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Requirements
Deep Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
    1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
    1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
    2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
    3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
    4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
    5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
    6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY
      1.2. Parent Memory Capability GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY This document shall refine the Memory Management capability defined by 50_memory_management.sdoc and shall preserve the authority boundaries defined by 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc and 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE
      1.3. ActiveBrief Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE ActiveRetrievalBrief requirements in this document shall cover creation, update, propagation, contributor tracking, relevance gating, and pending-topic handling performed during memory recording.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE
      1.4. Runtime Compression Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE Runtime compression requirements in this document shall cover query reduction and query-anchored reduction of selected episodic Q/A candidates without modifying durable source records.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE
      1.5. Memory Synthesis Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE Memory synthesis requirements in this document shall cover evidence formatting, Agent Stack invocation, structured output parsing, diagnostics, and write-back of synthesized Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP
      1.6. No Durable Truth Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP MemorySentenceReducer, MemoryCompressor, MemoryMergeSynthesizer, and the relevance gate shall not own .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or memory-vector truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE
      1.7. No Document Evidence Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE This document shall not assign document retrieval, reranking, A3 usefulness selection, A4 condensation, or S_CTX_MD ownership to Memory Management.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER
      1.8. No Final User Answer GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER ActiveBrief and Memory Context agents shall produce backend context artifacts and shall not answer the current user request.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP
      2.1. Implementation Location Map GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP The current implementation shall be traceable through these locations:
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_sentence_reducer.py: shared deterministic Q/A window reduction;
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_activebrief_relevance_gate.py: non-LLM ActiveBrief routing gate;
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_active_retrieval_brief.py: recording-time ActiveBrief orchestration;
      - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_compressor.py: query-time episodic compression;
      - ragstream/memory/memory_merge_synthesizer.py: runtime Memory Context synthesis;
      - data/agents/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer/: ActiveBrief init/update agent definitions;
      - data/agents/memory_synthesizer/memory_synthesizer_001.json: Memory Context agent definition;
      - ragstream/memory/memory_manager.py: recording-time invocation and durable write authority;
      - ragstream/retrieval/retriever_mem.py: retrieval-time invocation and SuperPrompt write-back;
      - ragstream/orchestration/super_prompt.py and superprompt_projector.py: runtime fields and rendering.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME
      2.2. Recording-Time Functional Group GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME ActiveBrief reduction, relevance gating, init/update agent execution, pending-topic maintenance, and stable ActiveBrief assignment shall execute as part of MemoryRecord capture before the new stable body is appended.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME
      2.3. Retrieval-Time Functional Group GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME Query anchoring, episodic compression, evidence assembly, Memory Context synthesis, and MemoryContextPack update shall execute as runtime retrieval functions after memory candidates have been selected.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER
      2.4. Shared Reducer Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER MemorySentenceReducer shall be the shared deterministic reduction component for recording-time centroid reduction and retrieval-time external-query-anchor reduction.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE
      2.5. Agent Stack Use GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE ActiveBrief and Memory Context LLM calls shall resolve versioned AgentPrompt definitions through AgentFactory and shall invoke the provider through LLMClient as defined by 20_agent_stack.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS
      2.6. State Ownership Map GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS MemoryManager shall own pending-topic RAM state and durable ActiveBrief assignment; MemoryContextPack shall own run-local candidate and synthesis results; SuperPrompt shall own run-local final memory fields.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM
      2.7. No UI Algorithm Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM Streamlit callbacks and views may trigger or display these functions but shall not own ActiveBrief routing, reduction, synthesis, or state-authority rules.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
        3.2. ActiveBrief Operational Title GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE Each non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief produced by the current summarizer contract shall be accompanied by active_retrieval_brief_title as an operational backend title.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION
        3.3. ActiveBrief Before Durable Append GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION MemoryManager.capture_pair shall attempt to build the new record ActiveBrief before appending that MemoryRecord stable body to .ragmem.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY
        3.4. MemoryManager Write Authority GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder shall return its result to MemoryManager and shall not write files, SQLite rows, or vectors directly.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY
        3.5. ActiveBrief Stable Body Fields GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY MemoryManager shall assign ActiveBrief title, body, and contributor IDs to the new MemoryRecord before persistence so those fields become part of the append-only .ragmem stable body.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE
        3.6. Query Independence GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE Recording-time ActiveBrief generation shall use the current record, prior clean ActiveBrief, record metadata, and pending-topic state and shall not use the later user retrieval query or document evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE
        3.7. No Prior Record Rewrite GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE Creating the ActiveBrief for record K shall not rewrite the stable body of earlier MemoryRecords.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE
        3.8. ActiveBrief Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE If ActiveBrief generation raises an exception inside MemoryManager, MemoryManager shall log the developer failure when enabled and shall continue the durable MemoryRecord capture path without fabricating a brief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF
        3.9. Imported Shared Brief Assignment GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF ChatGPT shared-link import may assign one user-supplied MemoryBrief title and body to every imported record; that assignment shall be distinguished from per-record ActiveBrief generation by the recording-time builder.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO
        3.10. Imported Records Without Supplied Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO When a ChatGPT shared-link import contains no supplied MemoryBrief, the current import path shall persist imported records with empty ActiveBrief fields rather than silently running the normal per-record ActiveBrief builder.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
        5.1. Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN The builder shall scan previous records from newest to oldest and shall select the first eligible non-empty ActiveBrief.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD
          5.2. Skip Black Source Record GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD A previous record whose current tag is Black shall not supply the previous clean ActiveBrief to the builder.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY
          5.3. Skip Empty Previous Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY A previous record with an empty active_retrieval_brief shall not be selected as the previous clean brief.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR
          5.4. Reject Brief with Black Contributor GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR The builder shall reject a previous ActiveBrief when any listed contributor ID belongs to a previous record currently tagged Black.
          • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK
            17.7. AC: Black Contributor Exclusion GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK Given a previous brief whose contributor is currently Black, the builder shall skip that brief when selecting previous clean continuity state.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK
          5.5. Contributor Fallback GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK When an otherwise eligible previous brief has no contributor IDs, the builder shall use the selected previous record ID as its contributor list.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS
          5.6. No Previous Clean Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS When no eligible previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall route the new record through initial ActiveBrief creation.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA
        5.7. Current Record Metadata Payload GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA The ActiveBrief agent metadata payload shall include record_id, parent_id, creation time, source, tag, retrieval source mode, active project, automatic keywords, user keywords, and embedded-file snapshot from the current MemoryRecord.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT
        6.1. ActiveBrief Relevance Gate GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT When enabled and a previous clean ActiveBrief exists, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate shall evaluate reduced current Q/A vectors against the previous ActiveBrief and optional pending-topic center before an ActiveBrief update agent is called.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS
          6.2. Reused Q/A Vectors GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS The gate shall reuse question_vectors and answer_vectors produced by MemorySentenceReducer and shall not re-embed the current Q/A solely for gating.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER
          6.3. ActiveBrief Center GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER The gate shall split the previous ActiveBrief into sentence windows, embed those windows, and calculate their compatible-vector centroid as the ActiveBrief comparison center.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS
          6.4. No-Previous Pass Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS When no previous ActiveBrief is supplied, the gate shall return a pass decision with route init_no_previous_activebrief.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA
          6.5. No-Q/A-Vector Skip Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA When a previous brief exists but current Q/A vectors are empty, the gate shall return should_update_activebrief false with route skip_no_qa_vectors.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS
          6.6. Missing ActiveBrief Center Safe Pass GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS When the gate cannot construct an ActiveBrief center, it shall return a safe pass with route activebrief_safe_pass rather than block the update.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES
          6.7. Q/A-to-Center Scores GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES The gate shall calculate cosine similarity between each current question/answer vector and the active comparison center.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL
          6.8. Top-Score Selection for Four or Fewer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL For one through four Q/A scores, the gate shall use all scores in its top-score mean.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM
          6.9. Top-Score Selection for Five through Twenty GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM For five through twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use the top four scores in its top-score mean.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE
          6.10. Top-Score Selection Above Twenty GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE For more than twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use floor(n divided by five), with at least one score, in its top-score mean.
          • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT
            17.9. AC: Gate Top-Score Counts GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT Gate tests at n equal to 4, 5, 20, 21, and 25 shall use 4, 4, 4, 4, and 5 top scores respectively.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS
          6.11. ActiveBrief Update Threshold GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS When the ActiveBrief comparison top-score mean is greater than or equal to activebrief_threshold, the gate shall route activebrief_update.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER
          6.12. Pending Topic Center GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER If the current Q/A fails the ActiveBrief threshold and a pending center exists, the gate shall evaluate current Q/A vectors against that pending-topic center using the same top-score rule.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT
          6.13. Pending Topic Shift Threshold GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT When the pending-topic comparison top-score mean is greater than or equal to pending_topic_threshold, the gate shall route pending_topic_shift.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP
          6.14. Skip and Update Pending Topic Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP When current Q/A fails both applicable thresholds, the gate shall route skip_and_update_pending_topic and shall not authorize the normal ActiveBrief update call.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS
          6.15. Gate Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS Gate diagnostics shall preserve route, reason, threshold values, score counts, selected top scores, and score summaries without requiring full vector logging.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM
          6.16. Gate Is Non-LLM GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM The relevance gate shall make its route deterministically from embeddings, thresholds, and pending state and shall not call an LLM.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT
        7.1. Initial Brief Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT If no previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall invoke the configured init agent using reduced current question, reduced current answer, current record metadata, and required-output text.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR
          7.2. Initial Contributor GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR The initial ActiveBrief contributor list shall contain the current record_id.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER
          7.3. Initial Route Clears Pending Buffer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER The initial ActiveBrief route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT
          17.3. AC: Initial ActiveBrief GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT Given a first captured record with reducible Q/A and a successful init agent response, the new record shall contain the returned title/body and contributor list containing only its record_id before .ragmem append.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED
        7.4. Gate Disabled Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED When a previous clean brief exists and the relevance gate is disabled, the builder shall invoke the update agent using the previous brief and current reduced Q/A and shall report route gate_disabled.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE
        7.5. Related ActiveBrief Update Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE For gate route activebrief_update, the builder shall invoke the update agent with the previous brief and current reduced Q/A.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED
          17.4. AC: Related ActiveBrief Update GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED Given a previous clean brief and current Q/A whose ActiveBrief top-score mean meets threshold, the update agent shall run, contributor IDs shall include the current record, and pending state shall clear.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS
        7.6. Safe-Pass Update Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS A gate safe-pass result authorizing update shall follow the builder update path and shall not be treated as a skipped Q/A.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS
        7.7. Update Contributor Merge GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS For a normal or gate-disabled update, contributor IDs shall be the ordered de-duplicated merge of prior contributor IDs and the current record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER
        7.8. Update Clears Pending Buffer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER A successful normal, safe-pass, or gate-disabled update route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT
        7.9. Confirmed Topic-Shift Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT For gate route pending_topic_shift, the builder shall combine pending reduced Q/A and current reduced Q/A and shall invoke the configured update agent with the previous ActiveBrief plus that combined reduced content.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS
          7.10. Topic-Shift Payload Labels GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS The combined topic-shift payload shall distinguish pending question, current question, pending answer, and current answer with the current explicit labels.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS
          7.11. Topic-Shift Contributor Merge GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS The topic-shift contributor list shall be the ordered de-duplicated merge of prior contributors, the pending record_id, and the current record_id.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER
          7.12. Topic Shift Clears Pending Buffer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER A confirmed topic-shift route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer after the agent call.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC
          17.6. AC: Confirmed Topic Shift GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC Given a pending skipped Q/A and a current Q/A meeting pending_topic_threshold while failing the old ActiveBrief threshold, the update agent payload shall include both labeled Q/A pairs and contributors shall include pending and current record IDs.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP
        7.13. Unrelated Q/A Skip Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP For a non-update gate route, the builder shall skip the ActiveBrief LLM call, copy the previous ActiveBrief title/body/contributor IDs into the new record result, and create a new pending-topic buffer from the current reduced Q/A.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG
          7.14. Skipped LLM Diagnostic GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG The skip route shall report activebrief_llm_skipped true and shall preserve the gate route in activebrief_gate_route.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP
          17.5. AC: Unrelated Q/A Skip GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP Given current Q/A that fails ActiveBrief and pending-topic thresholds, no ActiveBrief LLM call shall run, the previous brief shall be copied, and the current Q/A shall replace pending-topic state.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION
        7.15. No Fabricated ActiveBrief Fallback GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION If an init or update agent invocation or parse fails, the builder shall propagate the failure to MemoryManager and shall not invent an ActiveBrief result.
      • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE
        8.9. Contributor IDs as Stable Brief Trace GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE ActiveBrief contributor IDs shall identify the MemoryRecords whose accepted content contributed to the current stable brief.
        • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE
          8.10. Contributor De-Duplication GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE Contributor merge shall preserve first occurrence order and shall omit blank and duplicate record IDs.
        • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF
          8.11. Contributor Meaning Boundary GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF Contributor membership shall record builder traceability and shall not by itself assert sentence-level provenance for every phrase in an LLM-produced ActiveBrief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID
        9.1. ActiveBrief Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID The current ActiveBrief builder shall resolve agent_id memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer with distinct configured init and update version identifiers.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS
          9.2. Init Agent Bindings GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS The init agent shall bind reduced_question, reduced_answer, memory_record_metadata, and required_output and shall not require a previous ActiveBrief.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS
          9.3. Update Agent Bindings GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS The update agent shall bind previous_active_retrieval_brief, reduced_question, reduced_answer, memory_record_metadata, and required_output.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT
          9.4. ActiveBrief Output Schema GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT Both ActiveBrief agent definitions shall return JSON containing only active_retrieval_brief_title and active_retrieval_brief as declared output fields.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY
          9.5. No Separate Q/A Summary GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY The ActiveBrief agent contract shall not create a separate qa_summary field.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER
          9.6. No User-Facing Answer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER The ActiveBrief agents shall not answer the original request, speak to the user, or produce final answer text.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY
          9.7. Provided-Evidence Boundary GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY The ActiveBrief agents shall use only the supplied reduced Q/A, current metadata, and, for update, previous clean ActiveBrief and shall not import outside knowledge.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS
          9.8. Correction and Rejection Preservation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS The agent instructions shall treat explicit corrections as high-importance constraints and explicit rejected interpretations as negative constraints when present in supplied evidence.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION
          9.9. Emotional Wording Boundary GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION The agent instructions shall treat insults, frustration, short rejection, or emotional wording as feedback signals rather than automatically converting them into new topic content.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE
          9.10. Operational Title Rule GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE The current agent definitions shall request a neutral factual operational title of ten to twenty words describing the stable working-conversation center.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL
          9.11. Current ActiveBrief Model Configuration GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL The current init and update JSON definitions shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, JSON response format, and max_tokens 600.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL
          9.12. ActiveBrief LLM Invocation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL The builder shall compose the selected AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with the agent model and maximum output tokens, minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt cache key, then parse through AgentPrompt.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT
          9.13. Current ActiveBrief Limit Inconsistency GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT The current baseline shall record that runtime_config target_brief_tokens and builder required-output text use about 700 tokens while the current init/update agent hard rules specify an absolute maximum of 500 tokens.

          The implementation and requirements shall not claim one unified effective limit until this inconsistency is resolved in the versioned configuration and agent definitions.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG
        4.2. Reducer Configuration Fields GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG The reducer shall expose max_tokens_total, question_max_tokens, window_size_sentences, window_overlap_sentences, redundancy_threshold, and embedding_model as construction parameters.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT
        4.3. Sentence Segmentation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT The reducer shall split text using sentence-ending punctuation and line breaks and shall omit empty sentence units.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS
        4.4. Sentence Windows GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS The reducer shall form ordered sentence windows with window size at least one, overlap clamped below window size, and positive step equal to window size minus overlap.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED
        4.5. Always Vectorize Prepared Windows GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED The reducer shall embed prepared question and answer windows even when the original side is already within its token budget so the resulting vectors remain available to the relevance gate.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE
        4.6. Centroid Reduction Mode GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE reduce_with_centroid shall compute an anchor from the centroid of all compatible question and answer vectors and shall use that anchor for query-independent reduction.
        • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS
          4.8. No-Vector Centroid Result GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS When centroid mode has no Q/A vectors, the reducer shall return empty reduced text and vectors with a no_vectors diagnostic rather than fabricate content.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR
        4.7. External Anchor Reduction Mode GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR reduce_with_anchor shall reduce the prepared Q/A against a caller-supplied anchor vector and shall identify the diagnostic mode as external_anchor.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET
        4.9. Q/A Budget Allocation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET The reducer shall allocate the question share proportionally to original Q/A token estimates, cap it by question_max_tokens, provide a positive fallback for a non-empty question, and allocate the remaining total budget to the answer.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET
        4.10. Below-Budget Preservation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET When one side is already within its allocated budget, the reducer shall preserve that side text and all of its prepared vectors.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE
        4.11. Window Relevance Score GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE For a side requiring reduction, the reducer shall rank each compatible window by cosine similarity to the active anchor vector.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY
        4.12. Window Redundancy Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY The reducer shall skip a candidate window when its cosine similarity to any already selected window is greater than or equal to redundancy_threshold.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT
        4.13. Window Token Fit GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT The reducer shall skip a candidate window whose estimated tokens would cause selected window tokens to exceed the side budget.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK
        4.14. Top-Window Fallback GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK If no ranked window can be selected but scored windows exist, the reducer shall retain the highest-scoring window as the selection fallback.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER
        4.15. Original Sentence Order Restoration GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER The reducer shall reconstruct reduced text from selected sentence indexes in their original source order rather than relevance-rank order.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER
          17.8. AC: Reducer Order and Budget GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER Given over-budget Q/A with multiple windows, selected text shall remain within allocated budgets, redundant windows shall be excluded at threshold, and reconstructed sentences shall follow source order.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT
        4.16. Surviving Vector Output GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT When reduction occurs, returned question_vectors and answer_vectors shall correspond to the selected windows rather than all discarded windows.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE
        4.17. Bounded Text Slice Fallback GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE When over-budget text cannot be reconstructed from valid windows, the reducer shall use the current bounded character-slice fallback and shall not create new semantic content.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT
        4.18. Token Estimation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT The reducer shall use cl100k_base through tiktoken when available and shall otherwise estimate tokens as the ceiling of character count divided by four.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY
        4.19. Cosine Safety GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY Cosine comparison shall return zero for empty vectors, dimensional mismatch, or zero vector norms.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION
        4.20. Reducer Runtime Output Only GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION Reducer output shall not modify the original MemoryRecord input_text or output_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM
      8.1. RAM-Only Pending Topic State GHOST-MEM-PENDING-RAM MemoryManager.pending_activebrief_topic_buffer shall remain RAM-only and shall not be written to .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or the memory vector store.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CONTENT
        8.2. Pending Buffer Content GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CONTENT The pending buffer shall contain current record_id, creation time, reduced question, reduced answer, question vectors, answer vectors, centroid center_vector, and vector_count.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CENTER
        8.3. Pending Center Calculation GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CENTER The pending center_vector shall be the centroid of compatible current reduced question and answer vectors.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-REPLACEMENT
        8.4. Pending Buffer Replacement GHOST-MEM-PENDING-REPLACEMENT Each skip route shall replace the prior pending-topic buffer with the current skipped Q/A buffer returned by the builder.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-NEW
        8.5. Clear on New History GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-NEW Starting a new memory history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to an empty dictionary.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-LOAD
        8.6. Clear on History Load GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-LOAD Loading a memory history shall reset pending_activebrief_topic_buffer and shall not reconstruct it from persisted files.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-IMPORT
        8.7. Clear on Imported History GHOST-MEM-PENDING-CLEAR-IMPORT Persisting an imported ChatGPT history shall set pending_activebrief_topic_buffer to an empty dictionary.
      • GHOST-MEM-PENDING-NOT-BLACKBOARD
        8.8. Pending Buffer Is Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-PENDING-NOT-BLACKBOARD The pending-topic buffer shall not be described as a Blackboard because it is a single-purpose, active-history, RAM-only continuity aid without shared task coordination or multi-component posting semantics.
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT
      10.1. Runtime Memory Compressor GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT MemoryCompressor shall receive runtime configuration and shall produce query-dependent compressed fields without modifying durable MemoryRecord truth.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CONFIG
        10.2. Compression Configuration GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CONFIG MemoryCompressor shall consume the memory_compression section, including global enabled, effective-query maximum tokens and embedding model, and episodic reduction enablement, total budget, question budget, windowing, and redundancy threshold.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CURRENT-SCOPE
        10.3. Current Episodic-Only Compression GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-CURRENT-SCOPE The current compressor shall operate on selected episodic candidates only and shall not compress working-memory candidates, Direct Recall candidates, or semantic memory chunks in the current retrieval flow.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-DISABLED
        10.4. Compression Disabled Passthrough GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-DISABLED When global or episodic compression is disabled, compress_episodic_candidates shall return the candidate list without adding compressed fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-CLEAN
        10.5. Effective Query Normalization GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-CLEAN The compressor shall strip the effective retrieval query before reducing or embedding it.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-BELOW
        10.6. Below-Budget Query Preservation GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-BELOW When the effective query is within query_max_tokens, query reduction shall preserve it unchanged.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-ORDER
        10.7. Query Sentence Order GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-ORDER When the effective query exceeds its budget, deterministic query reduction shall inspect sentences in original order and append each sentence that fits the remaining budget.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-SKIP
        10.8. Oversized Query Sentence Skip GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-SKIP A query sentence that would exceed the configured query budget shall be skipped rather than partially included in the normal sentence-selection path.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-FALLBACK
        10.9. Query Fallback Slice GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-FALLBACK If no complete sentence fits, query reduction shall use the current bounded first-sentence character slice without generating new content.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-EMBED-ONCE
        10.10. Single Query Anchor Embedding GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-QUERY-EMBED-ONCE After deterministic query reduction, the compressor shall embed the reduced query once and shall use that vector as the external anchor for all selected episodic candidates in the run.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-ANCHOR
        10.11. Missing Query Anchor Passthrough GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-ANCHOR When no query anchor vector can be produced, the compressor shall return episodic candidates unchanged rather than fabricate compressed evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL
        10.12. Original Episodic Text Preservation GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL Compressed candidates shall retain original input_text and output_text and shall add compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text as runtime fields.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS
          17.10. AC: Query-Anchored Episodic Compression GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS Given enabled compression, a non-empty query anchor, and selected episodic candidates, each result shall preserve original Q/A and add query_anchor_episode compressed fields and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE
        10.13. Compression Mode Metadata GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE Each compressed episodic candidate shall identify memory_compression_mode as query_anchor_episode and shall include query and reducer diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE
        10.14. No Compression Persistence GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE MemoryCompressor shall not update .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or memory vectors with query-dependent compressed text.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG
        11.2. Synthesis Configuration GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG The synthesizer shall consume memory_merge_synthesizer enabled, agent_id, agent_version, target_context_tokens, max_episodic_records, max_semantic_chunks, and prompt_cache_key.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG
        11.3. Current Runtime Configuration Injection GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG The current MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryMergeSynthesizer with the active runtime_config dictionary so the normal retrieval path does not depend on the synthesizer fallback configuration loader.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED
        11.4. Disabled Synthesis GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED When synthesis is disabled, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with a disabled diagnostic.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP
        11.5. Episodic Input Cap GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_episodic_records candidates in their existing order.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP
        11.6. Semantic Chunk Input Cap GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_semantic_chunks in their existing order.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
        11.7. Current Synthesized Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES The current synthesizer evidence source shall include reduced episodic episodes and semantic memory chunks.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING
          11.8. Working Memory Not Synthesizer Input GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.working_memory_candidates as memory_evidence.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT
          11.9. Direct Recall Not Synthesizer Input GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.direct_recall_candidate as memory_evidence.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT
          17.11. AC: Current Synthesis Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT A synthesis payload shall include bounded episodic and semantic evidence but shall not include working-memory or Direct Recall candidates in memory_evidence under the current implementation.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT
        11.10. Episodic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT For each episodic candidate, evidence text shall identify episode index, record_id, optional tag/source/score, and available QUESTION and ANSWER blocks.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST
          11.11. Compressed Episodic Text Priority GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST Episodic evidence formatting shall use compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text when present and shall otherwise fall back to original input_text and output_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT
        11.12. Semantic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT For each semantic chunk, evidence text shall identify available vector_id, record_id, role, score, and TEXT content.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE
        11.13. No Evidence Skip GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE When the formatted episodic and semantic evidence text is empty, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with reason no_memory_evidence.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE
          17.12. AC: Empty Evidence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE With no formatted episodic or semantic evidence, no synthesis LLM call shall run and returned memory_context shall be empty with no_memory_evidence diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD
        11.14. Synthesis Payload GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD The synthesis payload shall contain effective_retrieval_query_text, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, memory_evidence, and required_output.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE
        11.15. ActiveBrief Reference Boundary GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be passed as orientation and anti-duplication reference and shall not be treated as a substitute for missing episodic or semantic evidence.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE
          11.16. Anti-Duplication Instruction Nature GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE ActiveBrief anti-duplication is currently an instruction to the synthesizer agent and shall not be represented as a deterministic overlap detector.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
        12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE
          12.2. Memory Synthesizer Agent Type GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE The current agent definition shall identify the agent as a Synthesizer named Memory Context Synthesizer.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL
          12.3. Current Memory Synthesizer Model GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL The current agent definition shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, max_tokens 1200, and JSON response format.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS
          12.4. Memory Synthesizer Bindings GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS The agent definition shall bind effective retrieval query, optional ActiveBrief title, optional ActiveBrief body, memory evidence, and required-output instructions.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT
          12.5. Memory Context Output Schema GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT The agent output schema shall declare one result field named memory_context and shall not declare a final answer field.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE
          12.6. Query-Relevant Context GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE The agent instructions shall retain only memory evidence connected to the effective retrieval query and shall return an empty memory_context when no useful evidence exists.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS
          12.7. Concrete Memory Fact Preservation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS When relevant, the agent instructions shall preserve concrete names, technical entities, decisions, constraints, corrections, and rejected interpretations from supplied memory evidence.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE
          12.8. Evidence De-Duplication Instruction GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE The agent instructions shall combine duplicate episodic and semantic evidence instead of repeating the same point.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION
          12.9. No Invented Memory GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION The agent shall not invent facts, preferences, project decisions, files, or implementation status and shall not import outside knowledge.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER
          12.10. Backend Context Only GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER The agent shall not answer the original request, address the user, or produce a final user-facing response.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL
          12.11. Memory Synthesis Invocation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall compose the configured AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt-cache key, and shall parse the returned content through AgentPrompt.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS
          12.12. Successful Synthesis Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS On successful parse, the synthesizer shall return stripped memory_context, synthesis diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped false.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS
          12.13. Successful Synthesis Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS Successful synthesis diagnostics shall include reason synthesized, configured target, selected input counts, output character count, provider usage, model name, status, and incomplete reason when available.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE
          12.14. Synthesis Failure Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE If composition, provider call, or parse raises an exception, the synthesizer shall return empty memory_context, a synthesis_failed diagnostic containing the error and input counts, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
          • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE
            17.13. AC: Synthesis Failure GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE When the Memory Context provider call or parse fails, the result shall contain empty memory_context, synthesis_failed diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION
          12.15. No Fabricated Context Fallback GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION A failed or skipped synthesis shall not be replaced with an invented deterministic Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK
      13.1. Pack Synthesis Assignment GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK After synthesis, MemoryRetriever shall store the returned memory_context and synthesis diagnostics through MemoryContextPack.set_synthesized_memory_context.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT
      13.2. SuperPrompt Memory Context Text GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT MemoryRetriever shall assign MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context to SuperPrompt.memory_context_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF
        17.14. AC: SuperPrompt Memory Handoff GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF After successful memory synthesis, SuperPrompt.memory_context_text shall equal MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context while document stage artifacts remain unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK
      13.3. SuperPrompt Raw Memory Pack GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK MemoryRetriever shall retain the full MemoryContextPack object and its dictionary/debug representations separately from memory_context_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF
      13.4. SuperPrompt ActiveBrief Snapshot GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF MemoryRetriever shall store the latest current eligible ActiveBrief title and body in the dedicated SuperPrompt fields and extras independently of synthesized Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER
      13.5. Normal Memory Context Rendering GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER SuperPromptProjector normal rendering shall use memory_context_text under the Memory Context section and shall not render raw MemoryContextPack debug Markdown by default.
      • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING
        13.6. Supporting Context Semantics GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING Rendered Memory Context shall be identified as supporting context and shall not override the current user task or explicit current constraints.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF-MODE
      13.7. ActiveBrief Projection Mode Dependency GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF-MODE Final ActiveBrief inclusion shall follow the deterministic final_prompt_mode and relation decision prepared by 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc rather than being independently reclassified by MemoryMerge.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION
      13.8. Memory Context Permission Dependency GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION Memory synthesis and final projection shall honor PreProcessing allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy; a request with memory context disabled shall not receive synthesized memory_context_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-PERMISSION
        17.15. AC: Memory Permission GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-PERMISSION When PreProcessing disables memory context, no synthesized memory context shall be rendered in the final SuperPrompt for that request.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-DOC-MUTATION
      13.9. No Document Context Mutation GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-DOC-MUTATION Memory synthesis and handoff shall not overwrite document S_CTX_MD, document stage views, or document selection IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-STAGE-CLAIM
      13.10. No Independent Pipeline Stage Claim GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-STAGE-CLAIM Current MemoryMerge write-back shall not claim a separate top-level document pipeline stage solely because compression and synthesis occur inside MemoryRetriever.run.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST
      13.11. No Runtime Context Persistence GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST memory_context_text, synthesis diagnostics, compressed episodic fields, and MemoryContextPack shall remain run-local artifacts and shall not be appended to .ragmem as new durable truth.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RUNTIME-ONLY
        17.16. AC: Runtime Artifact Non-Persistence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RUNTIME-ONLY After a retrieval run, compressed candidate fields and synthesized memory_context shall not appear as rewritten content in existing .ragmem records.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-RUNTIME
      14.1. Runtime Configuration Source GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-RUNTIME Normal application startup shall load ragstream/config/runtime_config.json and shall pass the resulting dictionary into MemoryRetriever and its retrieval-time compressor and synthesizer components.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LOAD
      14.2. Recording-Time ActiveBrief Configuration Load GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LOAD The current recording-time ActiveBrief builder, when created by MemoryManager without explicit configuration, shall load ragstream/config/runtime_config.json through its own configuration loader.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-REDUCER
      14.3. Consumed ActiveBrief Reducer Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-REDUCER Current ActiveBrief generation shall consume reducer max_tokens_total, question_max_tokens, window size, window overlap, redundancy threshold, and embedding model.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-GATE
      14.4. Consumed ActiveBrief Gate Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-GATE Current ActiveBrief generation shall consume gate enabled, activebrief_threshold, and pending_topic_threshold.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LLM
      14.5. Consumed ActiveBrief LLM Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LLM Current ActiveBrief generation shall consume agent_id, init agent version, update agent version, target_brief_tokens, and prompt_cache_key from the ActiveBrief LLM configuration section.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-COMPRESS
      14.6. Consumed Compression Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-COMPRESS Current runtime compression shall consume effective-query token limit and embedding model plus episodic enablement, total budget, question budget, window size, overlap, and redundancy threshold.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-SYNTH
      14.7. Consumed Synthesis Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-SYNTH Current MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall consume enabled, agent identity/version, target context tokens, episodic cap, semantic chunk cap, and prompt-cache key.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-AGENT-AUTHORITY
      14.8. Agent Definition Authority GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-AGENT-AUTHORITY Provider, model, temperature, provider maximum output tokens, dynamic bindings, static rules, and structured output schema shall remain owned by the corresponding versioned agent JSON definitions.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE
      14.9. No Hidden Configuration Override GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE A Python fallback value shall not be represented as JSON-controlled when the active runtime does not read the corresponding JSON field.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-CONFIG
        17.18. AC: Configuration Consumption Truth GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-CONFIG Changing a consumed memory transformation setting shall be observable in reduction, route, cap, or diagnostics; an unconsumed or contradictory field shall not be represented as effective without code evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-MIGRATION
      14.10. Operational Parameter Migration GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-MIGRATION Remaining tunable thresholds, token budgets, model identifiers, window parameters, and evidence caps shall be kept or migrated into validated versioned JSON rather than multiplied as unrelated hard-coded runtime values.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-VALIDATION
      14.11. Configuration Validation Requirement GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-VALIDATION Configuration loading shall reject or explicitly normalize invalid negative budgets, invalid window relationships, invalid threshold ranges, and missing required agent identities before those values silently alter memory behavior.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-DURABLE
      15.1. Transformation Failure Does Not Rewrite Truth GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-DURABLE A reducer, relevance-gate, ActiveBrief agent, compressor, or synthesis failure shall not rewrite prior .ragmem records, .ragmeta.json metadata, SQLite rows, or stored vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-EMPTY
      15.2. Empty Runtime Result Is Explicit GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-EMPTY A skipped or failed synthesis shall return empty memory_context with diagnostics rather than an ambiguous fabricated success.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-ACTIVE
      15.3. ActiveBrief Error Visibility GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-ACTIVE ActiveBrief builder failures shall be available to explicitly enabled developer logging even though current MemoryManager capture continues.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-PUBLIC
      15.4. Public Logging Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-PUBLIC Public logs shall not expose complete memory Q/A bodies, ActiveBrief bodies, synthesis evidence, provider messages, raw model output, or embedding vectors by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-DEVELOPER
      15.5. Developer Diagnostic Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-DEVELOPER Complete reduction, gate, agent payload, raw response, and synthesis snapshots shall remain behind explicitly enabled confidential developer logging.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-GATE
      15.6. Gate Log Summaries GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-GATE The gate log view shall report score summaries, counts, routes, and bounded text previews and shall not require full vector values.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-DETERMINISTIC-TEST
      15.7. Deterministic Component Testability GHOST-MEM-MERGE-DETERMINISTIC-TEST Sentence splitting, window formation, token allocation, centroid, cosine, redundancy filtering, top-score count, threshold routing, contributor merging, query reduction, and evidence formatting shall be independently testable.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-TEST
      15.8. Agent Contract Testability GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-TEST ActiveBrief and Memory Context agent definitions shall be testable for declared bindings, output fields, no-answer behavior, and rejection of undeclared output structure.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-REPRODUCIBILITY
      15.9. Decision Reproducibility GHOST-MEM-MERGE-REPRODUCIBILITY Diagnostics shall preserve configuration version or effective values, route, thresholds, selected counts, contributor IDs, and agent identity sufficiently to reproduce a transformation decision.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-OUTSIDE-KNOWLEDGE
      15.10. No Outside Knowledge in Memory Artifacts GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-OUTSIDE-KNOWLEDGE ActiveBrief and Memory Context outputs shall be grounded in supplied memory evidence and shall not add external facts solely from model knowledge.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-SEMANTIC-GUARANTEE
      15.11. LLM Compression Nature GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-SEMANTIC-GUARANTEE An LLM-produced ActiveBrief or memory_context shall be treated as a compressed runtime artifact and shall not be represented as a verbatim or complete copy of all source evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED
      16.1. No Current Blackboard Subsystem GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED The current Memory Management implementation shall not be described as providing a Blackboard coordinator, shared Blackboard schema, posting protocol, task queue, subscription mechanism, or conflict-resolution engine.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PACK-NOT
        16.2. MemoryContextPack Is Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PACK-NOT MemoryContextPack shall be described as a run-local typed candidate container and not as a Blackboard implementation.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-EXTRAS-NOT
        16.3. SuperPrompt Extras Are Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-EXTRAS-NOT SuperPrompt.extras shall be described as runtime auxiliary state and not as a Blackboard merely because multiple components write namespaced values into it.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PENDING-NOT
        16.4. Pending Topic State Is Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PENDING-NOT The ActiveBrief pending-topic buffer shall remain a private continuity state and shall not imply a general shared-agent workspace.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-BACKGROUND
        16.5. No Background Coordination Agents GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-BACKGROUND This document shall not claim current background agents that monitor, reconcile, or update memory, knowledge, requirements, architecture, code, or tests.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH
        16.6. No Current Knowledge Graph GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH This document shall not claim a current knowledge graph, graph traversal memory retrieval, graph persistence, or graph-based conflict resolution.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-CROSS-CONTEXT-DEDUPE
        16.7. No Knowledge-Memory Overlap Resolver GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-CROSS-CONTEXT-DEDUPE The current implementation shall not be described as deterministically comparing final Knowledge Context and Memory Context and removing cross-path overlap.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP
        16.8. Current Anti-Duplication Limit GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP The current ActiveBrief reference to MemoryMerge may guide the LLM away from repetition inside Memory Context; it shall not be treated as the future cross-path overlap-removal capability.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-FUTURE-OWNERSHIP
        16.9. Future Blackboard Ownership GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-FUTURE-OWNERSHIP Any future Blackboard, cross-context reconciliation, knowledge graph, background synchronization, or autonomous coordination capability shall require separately approved requirements and architecture, expected under the future-vision requirement family rather than inferred from this current document.
      • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-CURRENT-ACCEPTANCE
        16.10. Current Baseline Independence GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-CURRENT-ACCEPTANCE Acceptance of the current 50–53 Memory Management baseline shall not depend on implementation of a Blackboard, knowledge graph, background agent, or cross-path overlap resolver.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-BLACKBOARD
        17.17. AC: Blackboard Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-BLACKBOARD Inspection of the current baseline shall not identify MemoryContextPack, SuperPrompt.extras, or pending-topic state as satisfying a Blackboard protocol or shared coordination subsystem.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP
      17.1. Memory Merge Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemorySentenceReducer, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate, MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder, MemoryCompressor, MemoryMergeSynthesizer, and the associated memory agent JSON definitions shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNC
        17.19. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNC The 53 baseline shall not be accepted when recording position, reduction behavior, gate route, pending-state lifecycle, contributor trace, synthesis evidence classes, output schema, persistence boundary, or Blackboard status contradicts this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SIBLING-BOUNDARY
      17.2. Sibling Ownership Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SIBLING-BOUNDARY MemoryRecord and file persistence shall remain owned by 51; vector ingestion, candidate retrieval, scoring, and MemoryContextPack construction shall remain owned by 52.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY
    1.2. Parent Memory Capability GHOST-MEM-MERGE-PARENT-CAPABILITY This document shall refine the Memory Management capability defined by 50_memory_management.sdoc and shall preserve the authority boundaries defined by 51_memory_recording_and_files.sdoc and 52_memory_ingestion_and_retrieval.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE
    1.3. ActiveBrief Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ACTIVEBRIEF-SCOPE ActiveRetrievalBrief requirements in this document shall cover creation, update, propagation, contributor tracking, relevance gating, and pending-topic handling performed during memory recording.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE
    1.4. Runtime Compression Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-COMPRESSION-SCOPE Runtime compression requirements in this document shall cover query reduction and query-anchored reduction of selected episodic Q/A candidates without modifying durable source records.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE
    1.5. Memory Synthesis Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SYNTHESIS-SCOPE Memory synthesis requirements in this document shall cover evidence formatting, Agent Stack invocation, structured output parsing, diagnostics, and write-back of synthesized Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP
    1.6. No Durable Truth Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DURABLE-OWNERSHIP MemorySentenceReducer, MemoryCompressor, MemoryMergeSynthesizer, and the relevance gate shall not own .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or memory-vector truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE
    1.7. No Document Evidence Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-DOCUMENT-EVIDENCE This document shall not assign document retrieval, reranking, A3 usefulness selection, A4 condensation, or S_CTX_MD ownership to Memory Management.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
