        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER
    1.8. No Final User Answer GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-FINAL-ANSWER ActiveBrief and Memory Context agents shall produce backend context artifacts and shall not answer the current user request.

    2. 2. Implementation Location Map and Functional Decomposition
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP
    2.1. Implementation Location Map GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOCATION-MAP The current implementation shall be traceable through these locations:
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_sentence_reducer.py: shared deterministic Q/A window reduction;
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_activebrief_relevance_gate.py: non-LLM ActiveBrief routing gate;
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_active_retrieval_brief.py: recording-time ActiveBrief orchestration;
    - ragstream/memory/compression/memory_compressor.py: query-time episodic compression;
    - ragstream/memory/memory_merge_synthesizer.py: runtime Memory Context synthesis;
    - data/agents/memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer/: ActiveBrief init/update agent definitions;
    - data/agents/memory_synthesizer/memory_synthesizer_001.json: Memory Context agent definition;
    - ragstream/memory/memory_manager.py: recording-time invocation and durable write authority;
    - ragstream/retrieval/retriever_mem.py: retrieval-time invocation and SuperPrompt write-back;
    - ragstream/orchestration/super_prompt.py and superprompt_projector.py: runtime fields and rendering.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME
    2.2. Recording-Time Functional Group GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RECORDING-TIME ActiveBrief reduction, relevance gating, init/update agent execution, pending-topic maintenance, and stable ActiveBrief assignment shall execute as part of MemoryRecord capture before the new stable body is appended.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME
    2.3. Retrieval-Time Functional Group GHOST-MEM-MERGE-RETRIEVAL-TIME Query anchoring, episodic compression, evidence assembly, Memory Context synthesis, and MemoryContextPack update shall execute as runtime retrieval functions after memory candidates have been selected.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER
    2.4. Shared Reducer Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SHARED-REDUCER MemorySentenceReducer shall be the shared deterministic reduction component for recording-time centroid reduction and retrieval-time external-query-anchor reduction.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE
    2.5. Agent Stack Use GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-STACK-USE ActiveBrief and Memory Context LLM calls shall resolve versioned AgentPrompt definitions through AgentFactory and shall invoke the provider through LLMClient as defined by 20_agent_stack.sdoc.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS
    2.6. State Ownership Map GHOST-MEM-MERGE-STATE-OWNERS MemoryManager shall own pending-topic RAM state and durable ActiveBrief assignment; MemoryContextPack shall own run-local candidate and synthesis results; SuperPrompt shall own run-local final memory fields.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM
    2.7. No UI Algorithm Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-UI-ALGORITHM Streamlit callbacks and views may trigger or display these functions but shall not own ActiveBrief routing, reduction, synthesis, or state-authority rules.

    3. 3. ActiveRetrievalBrief Recording Position and Authority
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
    3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
      3.2. ActiveBrief Operational Title GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE Each non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief produced by the current summarizer contract shall be accompanied by active_retrieval_brief_title as an operational backend title.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION
      3.3. ActiveBrief Before Durable Append GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION MemoryManager.capture_pair shall attempt to build the new record ActiveBrief before appending that MemoryRecord stable body to .ragmem.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY
      3.4. MemoryManager Write Authority GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder shall return its result to MemoryManager and shall not write files, SQLite rows, or vectors directly.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY
      3.5. ActiveBrief Stable Body Fields GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY MemoryManager shall assign ActiveBrief title, body, and contributor IDs to the new MemoryRecord before persistence so those fields become part of the append-only .ragmem stable body.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE
      3.6. Query Independence GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE Recording-time ActiveBrief generation shall use the current record, prior clean ActiveBrief, record metadata, and pending-topic state and shall not use the later user retrieval query or document evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE
      3.7. No Prior Record Rewrite GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE Creating the ActiveBrief for record K shall not rewrite the stable body of earlier MemoryRecords.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE
      3.8. ActiveBrief Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE If ActiveBrief generation raises an exception inside MemoryManager, MemoryManager shall log the developer failure when enabled and shall continue the durable MemoryRecord capture path without fabricating a brief.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF
      3.9. Imported Shared Brief Assignment GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF ChatGPT shared-link import may assign one user-supplied MemoryBrief title and body to every imported record; that assignment shall be distinguished from per-record ActiveBrief generation by the recording-time builder.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO
      3.10. Imported Records Without Supplied Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO When a ChatGPT shared-link import contains no supplied MemoryBrief, the current import path shall persist imported records with empty ActiveBrief fields rather than silently running the normal per-record ActiveBrief builder.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
      5.1. Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN The builder shall scan previous records from newest to oldest and shall select the first eligible non-empty ActiveBrief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD
        5.2. Skip Black Source Record GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD A previous record whose current tag is Black shall not supply the previous clean ActiveBrief to the builder.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY
        5.3. Skip Empty Previous Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY A previous record with an empty active_retrieval_brief shall not be selected as the previous clean brief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR
        5.4. Reject Brief with Black Contributor GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR The builder shall reject a previous ActiveBrief when any listed contributor ID belongs to a previous record currently tagged Black.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK
          17.7. AC: Black Contributor Exclusion GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK Given a previous brief whose contributor is currently Black, the builder shall skip that brief when selecting previous clean continuity state.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK
        5.5. Contributor Fallback GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK When an otherwise eligible previous brief has no contributor IDs, the builder shall use the selected previous record ID as its contributor list.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS
        5.6. No Previous Clean Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS When no eligible previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall route the new record through initial ActiveBrief creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA
      5.7. Current Record Metadata Payload GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CURRENT-METADATA The ActiveBrief agent metadata payload shall include record_id, parent_id, creation time, source, tag, retrieval source mode, active project, automatic keywords, user keywords, and embedded-file snapshot from the current MemoryRecord.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT
      6.1. ActiveBrief Relevance Gate GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT When enabled and a previous clean ActiveBrief exists, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate shall evaluate reduced current Q/A vectors against the previous ActiveBrief and optional pending-topic center before an ActiveBrief update agent is called.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS
        6.2. Reused Q/A Vectors GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-QA-VECTORS The gate shall reuse question_vectors and answer_vectors produced by MemorySentenceReducer and shall not re-embed the current Q/A solely for gating.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER
        6.3. ActiveBrief Center GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-BRIEF-CENTER The gate shall split the previous ActiveBrief into sentence windows, embed those windows, and calculate their compatible-vector centroid as the ActiveBrief comparison center.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS
        6.4. No-Previous Pass Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-PREVIOUS When no previous ActiveBrief is supplied, the gate shall return a pass decision with route init_no_previous_activebrief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA
        6.5. No-Q/A-Vector Skip Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-QA When a previous brief exists but current Q/A vectors are empty, the gate shall return should_update_activebrief false with route skip_no_qa_vectors.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS
        6.6. Missing ActiveBrief Center Safe Pass GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SAFE-PASS When the gate cannot construct an ActiveBrief center, it shall return a safe pass with route activebrief_safe_pass rather than block the update.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES
        6.7. Q/A-to-Center Scores GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-COSINE-SCORES The gate shall calculate cosine similarity between each current question/answer vector and the active comparison center.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL
        6.8. Top-Score Selection for Four or Fewer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-SMALL For one through four Q/A scores, the gate shall use all scores in its top-score mean.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM
        6.9. Top-Score Selection for Five through Twenty GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-MEDIUM For five through twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use the top four scores in its top-score mean.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE
        6.10. Top-Score Selection Above Twenty GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE For more than twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use floor(n divided by five), with at least one score, in its top-score mean.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT
          17.9. AC: Gate Top-Score Counts GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT Gate tests at n equal to 4, 5, 20, 21, and 25 shall use 4, 4, 4, 4, and 5 top scores respectively.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS
        6.11. ActiveBrief Update Threshold GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ACTIVE-PASS When the ActiveBrief comparison top-score mean is greater than or equal to activebrief_threshold, the gate shall route activebrief_update.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER
        6.12. Pending Topic Center GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-PENDING-CENTER If the current Q/A fails the ActiveBrief threshold and a pending center exists, the gate shall evaluate current Q/A vectors against that pending-topic center using the same top-score rule.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT
        6.13. Pending Topic Shift Threshold GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOPIC-SHIFT When the pending-topic comparison top-score mean is greater than or equal to pending_topic_threshold, the gate shall route pending_topic_shift.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP
        6.14. Skip and Update Pending Topic Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-SKIP When current Q/A fails both applicable thresholds, the gate shall route skip_and_update_pending_topic and shall not authorize the normal ActiveBrief update call.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS
        6.15. Gate Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-DIAGNOSTICS Gate diagnostics shall preserve route, reason, threshold values, score counts, selected top scores, and score summaries without requiring full vector logging.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM
        6.16. Gate Is Non-LLM GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-NO-LLM The relevance gate shall make its route deterministically from embeddings, thresholds, and pending state and shall not call an LLM.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT
      7.1. Initial Brief Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT If no previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall invoke the configured init agent using reduced current question, reduced current answer, current record metadata, and required-output text.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR
        7.2. Initial Contributor GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-CONTRIBUTOR The initial ActiveBrief contributor list shall contain the current record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER
        7.3. Initial Route Clears Pending Buffer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT-BUFFER The initial ActiveBrief route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT
        17.3. AC: Initial ActiveBrief GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT Given a first captured record with reducible Q/A and a successful init agent response, the new record shall contain the returned title/body and contributor list containing only its record_id before .ragmem append.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED
      7.4. Gate Disabled Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-GATE-DISABLED When a previous clean brief exists and the relevance gate is disabled, the builder shall invoke the update agent using the previous brief and current reduced Q/A and shall report route gate_disabled.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE
      7.5. Related ActiveBrief Update Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE For gate route activebrief_update, the builder shall invoke the update agent with the previous brief and current reduced Q/A.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED
        17.4. AC: Related ActiveBrief Update GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED Given a previous clean brief and current Q/A whose ActiveBrief top-score mean meets threshold, the update agent shall run, contributor IDs shall include the current record, and pending state shall clear.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS
      7.6. Safe-Pass Update Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SAFE-PASS A gate safe-pass result authorizing update shall follow the builder update path and shall not be treated as a skipped Q/A.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS
      7.7. Update Contributor Merge GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-CONTRIBUTORS For a normal or gate-disabled update, contributor IDs shall be the ordered de-duplicated merge of prior contributor IDs and the current record_id.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER
      7.8. Update Clears Pending Buffer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-UPDATE-BUFFER A successful normal, safe-pass, or gate-disabled update route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT
      7.9. Confirmed Topic-Shift Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT For gate route pending_topic_shift, the builder shall combine pending reduced Q/A and current reduced Q/A and shall invoke the configured update agent with the previous ActiveBrief plus that combined reduced content.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS
        7.10. Topic-Shift Payload Labels GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-LABELS The combined topic-shift payload shall distinguish pending question, current question, pending answer, and current answer with the current explicit labels.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS
        7.11. Topic-Shift Contributor Merge GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-CONTRIBUTORS The topic-shift contributor list shall be the ordered de-duplicated merge of prior contributors, the pending record_id, and the current record_id.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER
        7.12. Topic Shift Clears Pending Buffer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-BUFFER A confirmed topic-shift route shall return an empty pending-topic buffer after the agent call.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC
        17.6. AC: Confirmed Topic Shift GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC Given a pending skipped Q/A and a current Q/A meeting pending_topic_threshold while failing the old ActiveBrief threshold, the update agent payload shall include both labeled Q/A pairs and contributors shall include pending and current record IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP
      7.13. Unrelated Q/A Skip Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP For a non-update gate route, the builder shall skip the ActiveBrief LLM call, copy the previous ActiveBrief title/body/contributor IDs into the new record result, and create a new pending-topic buffer from the current reduced Q/A.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG
        7.14. Skipped LLM Diagnostic GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP-LLM-FLAG The skip route shall report activebrief_llm_skipped true and shall preserve the gate route in activebrief_gate_route.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP
        17.5. AC: Unrelated Q/A Skip GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP Given current Q/A that fails ActiveBrief and pending-topic thresholds, no ActiveBrief LLM call shall run, the previous brief shall be copied, and the current Q/A shall replace pending-topic state.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION
      7.15. No Fabricated ActiveBrief Fallback GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NO-FABRICATION If an init or update agent invocation or parse fails, the builder shall propagate the failure to MemoryManager and shall not invent an ActiveBrief result.
    • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE
      8.9. Contributor IDs as Stable Brief Trace GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-STABLE ActiveBrief contributor IDs shall identify the MemoryRecords whose accepted content contributed to the current stable brief.
      • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE
        8.10. Contributor De-Duplication GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-UNIQUE Contributor merge shall preserve first occurrence order and shall omit blank and duplicate record IDs.
      • GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF
        8.11. Contributor Meaning Boundary GHOST-MEM-CONTRIBUTOR-NO-SEMANTIC-PROOF Contributor membership shall record builder traceability and shall not by itself assert sentence-level provenance for every phrase in an LLM-produced ActiveBrief.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID
      9.1. ActiveBrief Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-ID The current ActiveBrief builder shall resolve agent_id memory_activebrief_qa_summarizer with distinct configured init and update version identifiers.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS
        9.2. Init Agent Bindings GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-INIT-BINDINGS The init agent shall bind reduced_question, reduced_answer, memory_record_metadata, and required_output and shall not require a previous ActiveBrief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS
        9.3. Update Agent Bindings GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-UPDATE-BINDINGS The update agent shall bind previous_active_retrieval_brief, reduced_question, reduced_answer, memory_record_metadata, and required_output.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT
        9.4. ActiveBrief Output Schema GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-OUTPUT Both ActiveBrief agent definitions shall return JSON containing only active_retrieval_brief_title and active_retrieval_brief as declared output fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY
        9.5. No Separate Q/A Summary GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-QA-SUMMARY The ActiveBrief agent contract shall not create a separate qa_summary field.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER
        9.6. No User-Facing Answer GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-NO-ANSWER The ActiveBrief agents shall not answer the original request, speak to the user, or produce final answer text.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY
        9.7. Provided-Evidence Boundary GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-FACT-BOUNDARY The ActiveBrief agents shall use only the supplied reduced Q/A, current metadata, and, for update, previous clean ActiveBrief and shall not import outside knowledge.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS
        9.8. Correction and Rejection Preservation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CORRECTIONS The agent instructions shall treat explicit corrections as high-importance constraints and explicit rejected interpretations as negative constraints when present in supplied evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION
        9.9. Emotional Wording Boundary GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-EMOTION The agent instructions shall treat insults, frustration, short rejection, or emotional wording as feedback signals rather than automatically converting them into new topic content.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE
        9.10. Operational Title Rule GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-TITLE-RULE The current agent definitions shall request a neutral factual operational title of ten to twenty words describing the stable working-conversation center.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL
        9.11. Current ActiveBrief Model Configuration GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-MODEL The current init and update JSON definitions shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, JSON response format, and max_tokens 600.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL
        9.12. ActiveBrief LLM Invocation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-CALL The builder shall compose the selected AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with the agent model and maximum output tokens, minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt cache key, then parse through AgentPrompt.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT
        9.13. Current ActiveBrief Limit Inconsistency GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEAGENT-LIMIT-CONFLICT The current baseline shall record that runtime_config target_brief_tokens and builder required-output text use about 700 tokens while the current init/update agent hard rules specify an absolute maximum of 500 tokens.

        The implementation and requirements shall not claim one unified effective limit until this inconsistency is resolved in the versioned configuration and agent definitions.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE
    3.2. ActiveBrief Operational Title GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-TITLE Each non-empty ActiveRetrievalBrief produced by the current summarizer contract shall be accompanied by active_retrieval_brief_title as an operational backend title.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION
    3.3. ActiveBrief Before Durable Append GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-RECORDING-POSITION MemoryManager.capture_pair shall attempt to build the new record ActiveBrief before appending that MemoryRecord stable body to .ragmem.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY
    3.4. MemoryManager Write Authority GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-MANAGER-AUTHORITY MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder shall return its result to MemoryManager and shall not write files, SQLite rows, or vectors directly.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY
    3.5. ActiveBrief Stable Body Fields GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-STABLE-BODY MemoryManager shall assign ActiveBrief title, body, and contributor IDs to the new MemoryRecord before persistence so those fields become part of the append-only .ragmem stable body.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE
    3.6. Query Independence GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-QUERY-INDEPENDENCE Recording-time ActiveBrief generation shall use the current record, prior clean ActiveBrief, record metadata, and pending-topic state and shall not use the later user retrieval query or document evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE
    3.7. No Prior Record Rewrite GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-RECORD-REWRITE Creating the ActiveBrief for record K shall not rewrite the stable body of earlier MemoryRecords.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE
    3.8. ActiveBrief Failure Isolation GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CAPTURE-FAILURE If ActiveBrief generation raises an exception inside MemoryManager, MemoryManager shall log the developer failure when enabled and shall continue the durable MemoryRecord capture path without fabricating a brief.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF
    3.9. Imported Shared Brief Assignment GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORTED-BRIEF ChatGPT shared-link import may assign one user-supplied MemoryBrief title and body to every imported record; that assignment shall be distinguished from per-record ActiveBrief generation by the recording-time builder.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and

        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO
    3.10. Imported Records Without Supplied Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-IMPORT-NO-AUTO When a ChatGPT shared-link import contains no supplied MemoryBrief, the current import path shall persist imported records with empty ActiveBrief fields rather than silently running the normal per-record ActiveBrief builder.

    4. 4. MemorySentenceReducer Contract
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
    4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG
      4.2. Reducer Configuration Fields GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CONFIG The reducer shall expose max_tokens_total, question_max_tokens, window_size_sentences, window_overlap_sentences, redundancy_threshold, and embedding_model as construction parameters.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT
      4.3. Sentence Segmentation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SENTENCE-SPLIT The reducer shall split text using sentence-ending punctuation and line breaks and shall omit empty sentence units.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS
      4.4. Sentence Windows GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-WINDOWS The reducer shall form ordered sentence windows with window size at least one, overlap clamped below window size, and positive step equal to window size minus overlap.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED
      4.5. Always Vectorize Prepared Windows GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ALWAYS-EMBED The reducer shall embed prepared question and answer windows even when the original side is already within its token budget so the resulting vectors remain available to the relevance gate.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE
      4.6. Centroid Reduction Mode GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-CENTROID-MODE reduce_with_centroid shall compute an anchor from the centroid of all compatible question and answer vectors and shall use that anchor for query-independent reduction.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS
        4.8. No-Vector Centroid Result GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-VECTORS When centroid mode has no Q/A vectors, the reducer shall return empty reduced text and vectors with a no_vectors diagnostic rather than fabricate content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR
      4.7. External Anchor Reduction Mode GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-EXTERNAL-ANCHOR reduce_with_anchor shall reduce the prepared Q/A against a caller-supplied anchor vector and shall identify the diagnostic mode as external_anchor.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET
      4.9. Q/A Budget Allocation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET The reducer shall allocate the question share proportionally to original Q/A token estimates, cap it by question_max_tokens, provide a positive fallback for a non-empty question, and allocate the remaining total budget to the answer.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET
      4.10. Below-Budget Preservation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET When one side is already within its allocated budget, the reducer shall preserve that side text and all of its prepared vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE
      4.11. Window Relevance Score GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE For a side requiring reduction, the reducer shall rank each compatible window by cosine similarity to the active anchor vector.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY
      4.12. Window Redundancy Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY The reducer shall skip a candidate window when its cosine similarity to any already selected window is greater than or equal to redundancy_threshold.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT
      4.13. Window Token Fit GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT The reducer shall skip a candidate window whose estimated tokens would cause selected window tokens to exceed the side budget.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK
      4.14. Top-Window Fallback GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK If no ranked window can be selected but scored windows exist, the reducer shall retain the highest-scoring window as the selection fallback.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER
      4.15. Original Sentence Order Restoration GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER The reducer shall reconstruct reduced text from selected sentence indexes in their original source order rather than relevance-rank order.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER
        17.8. AC: Reducer Order and Budget GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER Given over-budget Q/A with multiple windows, selected text shall remain within allocated budgets, redundant windows shall be excluded at threshold, and reconstructed sentences shall follow source order.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT
      4.16. Surviving Vector Output GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT When reduction occurs, returned question_vectors and answer_vectors shall correspond to the selected windows rather than all discarded windows.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE
      4.17. Bounded Text Slice Fallback GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE When over-budget text cannot be reconstructed from valid windows, the reducer shall use the current bounded character-slice fallback and shall not create new semantic content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT
      4.18. Token Estimation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT The reducer shall use cl100k_base through tiktoken when available and shall otherwise estimate tokens as the ceiling of character count divided by four.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY
      4.19. Cosine Safety GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY Cosine comparison shall return zero for empty vectors, dimensional mismatch, or zero vector norms.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION
      4.20. Reducer Runtime Output Only GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION Reducer output shall not modify the original MemoryRecord input_text or output_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE

        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET
    4.9. Q/A Budget Allocation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BUDGET The reducer shall allocate the question share proportionally to original Q/A token estimates, cap it by question_max_tokens, provide a positive fallback for a non-empty question, and allocate the remaining total budget to the answer.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET
    4.10. Below-Budget Preservation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-BELOW-BUDGET When one side is already within its allocated budget, the reducer shall preserve that side text and all of its prepared vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE
    4.11. Window Relevance Score GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-SCORE For a side requiring reduction, the reducer shall rank each compatible window by cosine similarity to the active anchor vector.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY
    4.12. Window Redundancy Exclusion GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-REDUNDANCY The reducer shall skip a candidate window when its cosine similarity to any already selected window is greater than or equal to redundancy_threshold.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT
    4.13. Window Token Fit GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-FIT The reducer shall skip a candidate window whose estimated tokens would cause selected window tokens to exceed the side budget.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK
    4.14. Top-Window Fallback GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOP-FALLBACK If no ranked window can be selected but scored windows exist, the reducer shall retain the highest-scoring window as the selection fallback.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER
    4.15. Original Sentence Order Restoration GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER The reducer shall reconstruct reduced text from selected sentence indexes in their original source order rather than relevance-rank order.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER
      17.8. AC: Reducer Order and Budget GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER Given over-budget Q/A with multiple windows, selected text shall remain within allocated budgets, redundant windows shall be excluded at threshold, and reconstructed sentences shall follow source order.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT
    4.16. Surviving Vector Output GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-VECTOR-OUTPUT When reduction occurs, returned question_vectors and answer_vectors shall correspond to the selected windows rather than all discarded windows.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:

    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE
    4.17. Bounded Text Slice Fallback GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-FALLBACK-SLICE When over-budget text cannot be reconstructed from valid windows, the reducer shall use the current bounded character-slice fallback and shall not create new semantic content.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT
    4.18. Token Estimation GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-TOKEN-COUNT The reducer shall use cl100k_base through tiktoken when available and shall otherwise estimate tokens as the ceiling of character count divided by four.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY
    4.19. Cosine Safety GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-COSINE-SAFETY Cosine comparison shall return zero for empty vectors, dimensional mismatch, or zero vector norms.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
      4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;

          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION
    4.20. Reducer Runtime Output Only GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-NO-DURABLE-MUTATION Reducer output shall not modify the original MemoryRecord input_text or output_text.

    5. 5. ActiveBrief Previous-State Selection
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
      3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
    5.1. Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN The builder shall scan previous records from newest to oldest and shall select the first eligible non-empty ActiveBrief.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD
      5.2. Skip Black Source Record GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-BLACK-RECORD A previous record whose current tag is Black shall not supply the previous clean ActiveBrief to the builder.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY
      5.3. Skip Empty Previous Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-SKIP-EMPTY A previous record with an empty active_retrieval_brief shall not be selected as the previous clean brief.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR
      5.4. Reject Brief with Black Contributor GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR The builder shall reject a previous ActiveBrief when any listed contributor ID belongs to a previous record currently tagged Black.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK
        17.7. AC: Black Contributor Exclusion GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK Given a previous brief whose contributor is currently Black, the builder shall skip that brief when selecting previous clean continuity state.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK
      5.5. Contributor Fallback GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-CONTRIBUTOR-FALLBACK When an otherwise eligible previous brief has no contributor IDs, the builder shall use the selected previous record ID as its contributor list.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS
      5.6. No Previous Clean Brief GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-NO-PREVIOUS When no eligible previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall route the new record through initial ActiveBrief creation.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
      5.1. Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN The builder shall scan previous records from newest to oldest and shall select the first eligible non-empty ActiveBrief.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
        3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
