          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL
    10.12. Original Episodic Text Preservation GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL Compressed candidates shall retain original input_text and output_text and shall add compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text as runtime fields.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS
      17.10. AC: Query-Anchored Episodic Compression GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS Given enabled compression, a non-empty query anchor, and selected episodic candidates, each result shall preserve original Q/A and add query_anchor_episode compressed fields and diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT
      10.1. Runtime Memory Compressor GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT MemoryCompressor shall receive runtime configuration and shall produce query-dependent compressed fields without modifying durable MemoryRecord truth.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE
    10.13. Compression Mode Metadata GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-MODE Each compressed episodic candidate shall identify memory_compression_mode as query_anchor_episode and shall include query and reducer diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT
      10.1. Runtime Memory Compressor GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT MemoryCompressor shall receive runtime configuration and shall produce query-dependent compressed fields without modifying durable MemoryRecord truth.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE
    10.14. No Compression Persistence GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-NO-STORE-WRITE MemoryCompressor shall not update .ragmem, .ragmeta.json, SQLite, or memory vectors with query-dependent compressed text.

    11. 11. MemoryMergeSynthesizer Inputs and Evidence Construction
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
    11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG
      11.2. Synthesis Configuration GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG The synthesizer shall consume memory_merge_synthesizer enabled, agent_id, agent_version, target_context_tokens, max_episodic_records, max_semantic_chunks, and prompt_cache_key.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG
      11.3. Current Runtime Configuration Injection GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG The current MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryMergeSynthesizer with the active runtime_config dictionary so the normal retrieval path does not depend on the synthesizer fallback configuration loader.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED
      11.4. Disabled Synthesis GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED When synthesis is disabled, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with a disabled diagnostic.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP
      11.5. Episodic Input Cap GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_episodic_records candidates in their existing order.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP
      11.6. Semantic Chunk Input Cap GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_semantic_chunks in their existing order.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
      11.7. Current Synthesized Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES The current synthesizer evidence source shall include reduced episodic episodes and semantic memory chunks.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING
        11.8. Working Memory Not Synthesizer Input GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.working_memory_candidates as memory_evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT
        11.9. Direct Recall Not Synthesizer Input GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.direct_recall_candidate as memory_evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT
        17.11. AC: Current Synthesis Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT A synthesis payload shall include bounded episodic and semantic evidence but shall not include working-memory or Direct Recall candidates in memory_evidence under the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT
      11.10. Episodic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT For each episodic candidate, evidence text shall identify episode index, record_id, optional tag/source/score, and available QUESTION and ANSWER blocks.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST
        11.11. Compressed Episodic Text Priority GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST Episodic evidence formatting shall use compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text when present and shall otherwise fall back to original input_text and output_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT
      11.12. Semantic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT For each semantic chunk, evidence text shall identify available vector_id, record_id, role, score, and TEXT content.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE
      11.13. No Evidence Skip GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE When the formatted episodic and semantic evidence text is empty, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with reason no_memory_evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE
        17.12. AC: Empty Evidence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE With no formatted episodic or semantic evidence, no synthesis LLM call shall run and returned memory_context shall be empty with no_memory_evidence diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD
      11.14. Synthesis Payload GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD The synthesis payload shall contain effective_retrieval_query_text, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, memory_evidence, and required_output.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE
      11.15. ActiveBrief Reference Boundary GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be passed as orientation and anti-duplication reference and shall not be treated as a substitute for missing episodic or semantic evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE
        11.16. Anti-Duplication Instruction Nature GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE ActiveBrief anti-duplication is currently an instruction to the synthesizer agent and shall not be represented as a deterministic overlap detector.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE
        12.2. Memory Synthesizer Agent Type GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE The current agent definition shall identify the agent as a Synthesizer named Memory Context Synthesizer.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL
        12.3. Current Memory Synthesizer Model GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL The current agent definition shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, max_tokens 1200, and JSON response format.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS
        12.4. Memory Synthesizer Bindings GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS The agent definition shall bind effective retrieval query, optional ActiveBrief title, optional ActiveBrief body, memory evidence, and required-output instructions.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT
        12.5. Memory Context Output Schema GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT The agent output schema shall declare one result field named memory_context and shall not declare a final answer field.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE
        12.6. Query-Relevant Context GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE The agent instructions shall retain only memory evidence connected to the effective retrieval query and shall return an empty memory_context when no useful evidence exists.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS
        12.7. Concrete Memory Fact Preservation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS When relevant, the agent instructions shall preserve concrete names, technical entities, decisions, constraints, corrections, and rejected interpretations from supplied memory evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE
        12.8. Evidence De-Duplication Instruction GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE The agent instructions shall combine duplicate episodic and semantic evidence instead of repeating the same point.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION
        12.9. No Invented Memory GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION The agent shall not invent facts, preferences, project decisions, files, or implementation status and shall not import outside knowledge.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER
        12.10. Backend Context Only GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER The agent shall not answer the original request, address the user, or produce a final user-facing response.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL
        12.11. Memory Synthesis Invocation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall compose the configured AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt-cache key, and shall parse the returned content through AgentPrompt.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS
        12.12. Successful Synthesis Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS On successful parse, the synthesizer shall return stripped memory_context, synthesis diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped false.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS
        12.13. Successful Synthesis Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS Successful synthesis diagnostics shall include reason synthesized, configured target, selected input counts, output character count, provider usage, model name, status, and incomplete reason when available.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE
        12.14. Synthesis Failure Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE If composition, provider call, or parse raises an exception, the synthesizer shall return empty memory_context, a synthesis_failed diagnostic containing the error and input counts, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE
          17.13. AC: Synthesis Failure GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE When the Memory Context provider call or parse fails, the result shall contain empty memory_context, synthesis_failed diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION
        12.15. No Fabricated Context Fallback GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION A failed or skipped synthesis shall not be replaced with an invented deterministic Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG
    11.2. Synthesis Configuration GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CONFIG The synthesizer shall consume memory_merge_synthesizer enabled, agent_id, agent_version, target_context_tokens, max_episodic_records, max_semantic_chunks, and prompt_cache_key.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG
    11.3. Current Runtime Configuration Injection GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EXPLICIT-CONFIG The current MemoryRetriever shall construct MemoryMergeSynthesizer with the active runtime_config dictionary so the normal retrieval path does not depend on the synthesizer fallback configuration loader.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT

        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED
    11.4. Disabled Synthesis GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-DISABLED When synthesis is disabled, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with a disabled diagnostic.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP
    11.5. Episodic Input Cap GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-CAP Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_episodic_records candidates in their existing order.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP
    11.6. Semantic Chunk Input Cap GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-CAP Before evidence formatting, the synthesizer shall retain at most the first max_semantic_chunks in their existing order.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
    11.7. Current Synthesized Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES The current synthesizer evidence source shall include reduced episodic episodes and semantic memory chunks.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING
      11.8. Working Memory Not Synthesizer Input GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-WORKING The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.working_memory_candidates as memory_evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT
      11.9. Direct Recall Not Synthesizer Input GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-DIRECT The current synthesizer shall not receive MemoryContextPack.direct_recall_candidate as memory_evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT
      17.11. AC: Current Synthesis Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT A synthesis payload shall include bounded episodic and semantic evidence but shall not include working-memory or Direct Recall candidates in memory_evidence under the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
      11.7. Current Synthesized Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES The current synthesizer evidence source shall include reduced episodic episodes and semantic memory chunks.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT
    11.10. Episodic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT For each episodic candidate, evidence text shall identify episode index, record_id, optional tag/source/score, and available QUESTION and ANSWER blocks.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST
      11.11. Compressed Episodic Text Priority GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-COMPRESSED-FIRST Episodic evidence formatting shall use compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text when present and shall otherwise fall back to original input_text and output_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT
      11.10. Episodic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-EPISODIC-FORMAT For each episodic candidate, evidence text shall identify episode index, record_id, optional tag/source/score, and available QUESTION and ANSWER blocks.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT
    11.12. Semantic Evidence Format GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-SEMANTIC-FORMAT For each semantic chunk, evidence text shall identify available vector_id, record_id, role, score, and TEXT content.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE
    11.13. No Evidence Skip GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE When the formatted episodic and semantic evidence text is empty, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with reason no_memory_evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE
      17.12. AC: Empty Evidence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE With no formatted episodic or semantic evidence, no synthesis LLM call shall run and returned memory_context shall be empty with no_memory_evidence diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD
    11.14. Synthesis Payload GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-PAYLOAD The synthesis payload shall contain effective_retrieval_query_text, active_retrieval_brief_title, active_retrieval_brief, memory_evidence, and required_output.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE
    11.15. ActiveBrief Reference Boundary GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be passed as orientation and anti-duplication reference and shall not be treated as a substitute for missing episodic or semantic evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE
      11.16. Anti-Duplication Instruction Nature GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE ActiveBrief anti-duplication is currently an instruction to the synthesizer agent and shall not be represented as a deterministic overlap detector.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE
      11.15. ActiveBrief Reference Boundary GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ACTIVEBRIEF-REFERENCE ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be passed as orientation and anti-duplication reference and shall not be treated as a substitute for missing episodic or semantic evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
        11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;

          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE
    11.16. Anti-Duplication Instruction Nature GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ANTI-DUP-NATURE ActiveBrief anti-duplication is currently an instruction to the synthesizer agent and shall not be represented as a deterministic overlap detector.

    12. 12. Memory Synthesizer Agent Contract and Output
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
      11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
    12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE
      12.2. Memory Synthesizer Agent Type GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE The current agent definition shall identify the agent as a Synthesizer named Memory Context Synthesizer.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL
      12.3. Current Memory Synthesizer Model GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL The current agent definition shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, max_tokens 1200, and JSON response format.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS
      12.4. Memory Synthesizer Bindings GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS The agent definition shall bind effective retrieval query, optional ActiveBrief title, optional ActiveBrief body, memory evidence, and required-output instructions.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT
      12.5. Memory Context Output Schema GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT The agent output schema shall declare one result field named memory_context and shall not declare a final answer field.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE
      12.6. Query-Relevant Context GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE The agent instructions shall retain only memory evidence connected to the effective retrieval query and shall return an empty memory_context when no useful evidence exists.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS
      12.7. Concrete Memory Fact Preservation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS When relevant, the agent instructions shall preserve concrete names, technical entities, decisions, constraints, corrections, and rejected interpretations from supplied memory evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE
      12.8. Evidence De-Duplication Instruction GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE The agent instructions shall combine duplicate episodic and semantic evidence instead of repeating the same point.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION
      12.9. No Invented Memory GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION The agent shall not invent facts, preferences, project decisions, files, or implementation status and shall not import outside knowledge.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER
      12.10. Backend Context Only GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER The agent shall not answer the original request, address the user, or produce a final user-facing response.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL
      12.11. Memory Synthesis Invocation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-CALL MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall compose the configured AgentPrompt, call LLMClient.responses with minimal reasoning effort, metadata return enabled, and the configured prompt-cache key, and shall parse the returned content through AgentPrompt.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS
      12.12. Successful Synthesis Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-SUCCESS On successful parse, the synthesizer shall return stripped memory_context, synthesis diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped false.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS
      12.13. Successful Synthesis Diagnostics GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DIAGNOSTICS Successful synthesis diagnostics shall include reason synthesized, configured target, selected input counts, output character count, provider usage, model name, status, and incomplete reason when available.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE
      12.14. Synthesis Failure Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE If composition, provider call, or parse raises an exception, the synthesizer shall return empty memory_context, a synthesis_failed diagnostic containing the error and input counts, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE
        17.13. AC: Synthesis Failure GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE When the Memory Context provider call or parse fails, the result shall contain empty memory_context, synthesis_failed diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION
      12.15. No Fabricated Context Fallback GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION A failed or skipped synthesis shall not be replaced with an invented deterministic Memory Context.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
        11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE
    12.2. Memory Synthesizer Agent Type GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-TYPE The current agent definition shall identify the agent as a Synthesizer named Memory Context Synthesizer.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
        11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL
    12.3. Current Memory Synthesizer Model GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-MODEL The current agent definition shall configure provider openai, model gpt-5-mini, temperature zero, max_tokens 1200, and JSON response format.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS
    12.4. Memory Synthesizer Bindings GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-BINDINGS The agent definition shall bind effective retrieval query, optional ActiveBrief title, optional ActiveBrief body, memory evidence, and required-output instructions.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT
    12.5. Memory Context Output Schema GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-OUTPUT The agent output schema shall declare one result field named memory_context and shall not declare a final answer field.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE
    12.6. Query-Relevant Context GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-QUERY-RELEVANCE The agent instructions shall retain only memory evidence connected to the effective retrieval query and shall return an empty memory_context when no useful evidence exists.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
      12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS
    12.7. Concrete Memory Fact Preservation GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-PRESERVE-FACTS When relevant, the agent instructions shall preserve concrete names, technical entities, decisions, constraints, corrections, and rejected interpretations from supplied memory evidence.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE
    12.8. Evidence De-Duplication Instruction GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-DEDUPE The agent instructions shall combine duplicate episodic and semantic evidence instead of repeating the same point.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION
    12.9. No Invented Memory GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-INVENTION The agent shall not invent facts, preferences, project decisions, files, or implementation status and shall not import outside knowledge.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER
    12.10. Backend Context Only GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-ANSWER The agent shall not answer the original request, address the user, or produce a final user-facing response.

    GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION
    12.15. No Fabricated Context Fallback GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-NO-FABRICATION A failed or skipped synthesis shall not be replaced with an invented deterministic Memory Context.

    13. 13. MemoryContextPack and SuperPrompt Handoff
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK
    13.1. Pack Synthesis Assignment GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PACK After synthesis, MemoryRetriever shall store the returned memory_context and synthesis diagnostics through MemoryContextPack.set_synthesized_memory_context.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;

      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT
    13.2. SuperPrompt Memory Context Text GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT MemoryRetriever shall assign MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context to SuperPrompt.memory_context_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF
      17.14. AC: SuperPrompt Memory Handoff GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF After successful memory synthesis, SuperPrompt.memory_context_text shall equal MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context while document stage artifacts remain unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK
    13.3. SuperPrompt Raw Memory Pack GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-PACK MemoryRetriever shall retain the full MemoryContextPack object and its dictionary/debug representations separately from memory_context_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF
    13.4. SuperPrompt ActiveBrief Snapshot GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF MemoryRetriever shall store the latest current eligible ActiveBrief title and body in the dedicated SuperPrompt fields and extras independently of synthesized Memory Context.

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER
    13.5. Normal Memory Context Rendering GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER SuperPromptProjector normal rendering shall use memory_context_text under the Memory Context section and shall not render raw MemoryContextPack debug Markdown by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING
      13.6. Supporting Context Semantics GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SUPPORTING Rendered Memory Context shall be identified as supporting context and shall not override the current user task or explicit current constraints.

      13.5. Normal Memory Context Rendering GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-RENDER SuperPromptProjector normal rendering shall use memory_context_text under the Memory Context section and shall not render raw MemoryContextPack debug Markdown by default.

    13.7. ActiveBrief Projection Mode Dependency GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-ACTIVEBRIEF-MODE Final ActiveBrief inclusion shall follow the deterministic final_prompt_mode and relation decision prepared by 30_preprocessing_prompt_functions.sdoc rather than being independently reclassified by MemoryMerge.

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION
    13.8. Memory Context Permission Dependency GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION Memory synthesis and final projection shall honor PreProcessing allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy; a request with memory context disabled shall not receive synthesized memory_context_text.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-PERMISSION
      17.15. AC: Memory Permission GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-PERMISSION When PreProcessing disables memory context, no synthesized memory context shall be rendered in the final SuperPrompt for that request.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-DOC-MUTATION
    13.9. No Document Context Mutation GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-DOC-MUTATION Memory synthesis and handoff shall not overwrite document S_CTX_MD, document stage views, or document selection IDs.

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-STAGE-CLAIM
    13.10. No Independent Pipeline Stage Claim GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-STAGE-CLAIM Current MemoryMerge write-back shall not claim a separate top-level document pipeline stage solely because compression and synthesis occur inside MemoryRetriever.run.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:

    GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST
    13.11. No Runtime Context Persistence GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST memory_context_text, synthesis diagnostics, compressed episodic fields, and MemoryContextPack shall remain run-local artifacts and shall not be appended to .ragmem as new durable truth.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RUNTIME-ONLY
      17.16. AC: Runtime Artifact Non-Persistence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RUNTIME-ONLY After a retrieval run, compressed candidate fields and synthesized memory_context shall not appear as rewritten content in existing .ragmem records.

    14. 14. Configuration Governance and Current Consumption

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-RUNTIME
    14.1. Runtime Configuration Source GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-RUNTIME Normal application startup shall load ragstream/config/runtime_config.json and shall pass the resulting dictionary into MemoryRetriever and its retrieval-time compressor and synthesizer components.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LOAD
    14.2. Recording-Time ActiveBrief Configuration Load GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-ACTIVE-LOAD The current recording-time ActiveBrief builder, when created by MemoryManager without explicit configuration, shall load ragstream/config/runtime_config.json through its own configuration loader.

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-COMPRESS
    14.6. Consumed Compression Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-COMPRESS Current runtime compression shall consume effective-query token limit and embedding model plus episodic enablement, total budget, question budget, window size, overlap, and redundancy threshold.

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-SYNTH
    14.7. Consumed Synthesis Settings GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-SYNTH Current MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall consume enabled, agent identity/version, target context tokens, episodic cap, semantic chunk cap, and prompt-cache key.

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-AGENT-AUTHORITY
    14.8. Agent Definition Authority GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-AGENT-AUTHORITY Provider, model, temperature, provider maximum output tokens, dynamic bindings, static rules, and structured output schema shall remain owned by the corresponding versioned agent JSON definitions.

      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE
    14.9. No Hidden Configuration Override GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE A Python fallback value shall not be represented as JSON-controlled when the active runtime does not read the corresponding JSON field.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-CONFIG
      17.18. AC: Configuration Consumption Truth GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-CONFIG Changing a consumed memory transformation setting shall be observable in reduction, route, cap, or diagnostics; an unconsumed or contradictory field shall not be represented as effective without code evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-MIGRATION
    14.10. Operational Parameter Migration GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-MIGRATION Remaining tunable thresholds, token budgets, model identifiers, window parameters, and evidence caps shall be kept or migrated into validated versioned JSON rather than multiplied as unrelated hard-coded runtime values.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;

    GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-VALIDATION
    14.11. Configuration Validation Requirement GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-VALIDATION Configuration loading shall reject or explicitly normalize invalid negative budgets, invalid window relationships, invalid threshold ranges, and missing required agent identities before those values silently alter memory behavior.

    15. 15. Error Handling, Logging, and Quality Requirements
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-DURABLE
    15.1. Transformation Failure Does Not Rewrite Truth GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-DURABLE A reducer, relevance-gate, ActiveBrief agent, compressor, or synthesis failure shall not rewrite prior .ragmem records, .ragmeta.json metadata, SQLite rows, or stored vectors.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-EMPTY
    15.2. Empty Runtime Result Is Explicit GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-EMPTY A skipped or failed synthesis shall return empty memory_context with diagnostics rather than an ambiguous fabricated success.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-ACTIVE
    15.3. ActiveBrief Error Visibility GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ERROR-ACTIVE ActiveBrief builder failures shall be available to explicitly enabled developer logging even though current MemoryManager capture continues.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-PUBLIC
    15.4. Public Logging Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-PUBLIC Public logs shall not expose complete memory Q/A bodies, ActiveBrief bodies, synthesis evidence, provider messages, raw model output, or embedding vectors by default.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-DEVELOPER
    15.5. Developer Diagnostic Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-DEVELOPER Complete reduction, gate, agent payload, raw response, and synthesis snapshots shall remain behind explicitly enabled confidential developer logging.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-GATE
    15.6. Gate Log Summaries GHOST-MEM-MERGE-LOG-GATE The gate log view shall report score summaries, counts, routes, and bounded text previews and shall not require full vector values.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-DETERMINISTIC-TEST
    15.7. Deterministic Component Testability GHOST-MEM-MERGE-DETERMINISTIC-TEST Sentence splitting, window formation, token allocation, centroid, cosine, redundancy filtering, top-score count, threshold routing, contributor merging, query reduction, and evidence formatting shall be independently testable.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-TEST
    15.8. Agent Contract Testability GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AGENT-TEST ActiveBrief and Memory Context agent definitions shall be testable for declared bindings, output fields, no-answer behavior, and rejection of undeclared output structure.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-REPRODUCIBILITY
    15.9. Decision Reproducibility GHOST-MEM-MERGE-REPRODUCIBILITY Diagnostics shall preserve configuration version or effective values, route, thresholds, selected counts, contributor IDs, and agent identity sufficiently to reproduce a transformation decision.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-OUTSIDE-KNOWLEDGE
    15.10. No Outside Knowledge in Memory Artifacts GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-OUTSIDE-KNOWLEDGE ActiveBrief and Memory Context outputs shall be grounded in supplied memory evidence and shall not add external facts solely from model knowledge.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-SEMANTIC-GUARANTEE
    15.11. LLM Compression Nature GHOST-MEM-MERGE-NO-SEMANTIC-GUARANTEE An LLM-produced ActiveBrief or memory_context shall be treated as a compressed runtime artifact and shall not be represented as a verbatim or complete copy of all source evidence.

    16. 16. Blackboard and Future Development Boundary
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED
    16.1. No Current Blackboard Subsystem GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED The current Memory Management implementation shall not be described as providing a Blackboard coordinator, shared Blackboard schema, posting protocol, task queue, subscription mechanism, or conflict-resolution engine.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PACK-NOT
      16.2. MemoryContextPack Is Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PACK-NOT MemoryContextPack shall be described as a run-local typed candidate container and not as a Blackboard implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-EXTRAS-NOT
      16.3. SuperPrompt Extras Are Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-EXTRAS-NOT SuperPrompt.extras shall be described as runtime auxiliary state and not as a Blackboard merely because multiple components write namespaced values into it.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PENDING-NOT
      16.4. Pending Topic State Is Not Blackboard GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-PENDING-NOT The ActiveBrief pending-topic buffer shall remain a private continuity state and shall not imply a general shared-agent workspace.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-BACKGROUND
      16.5. No Background Coordination Agents GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-BACKGROUND This document shall not claim current background agents that monitor, reconcile, or update memory, knowledge, requirements, architecture, code, or tests.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH
      16.6. No Current Knowledge Graph GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH This document shall not claim a current knowledge graph, graph traversal memory retrieval, graph persistence, or graph-based conflict resolution.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-CROSS-CONTEXT-DEDUPE
      16.7. No Knowledge-Memory Overlap Resolver GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NO-CROSS-CONTEXT-DEDUPE The current implementation shall not be described as deterministically comparing final Knowledge Context and Memory Context and removing cross-path overlap.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP
      16.8. Current Anti-Duplication Limit GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-ACTIVEBRIEF-ANTI-DUP The current ActiveBrief reference to MemoryMerge may guide the LLM away from repetition inside Memory Context; it shall not be treated as the future cross-path overlap-removal capability.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-FUTURE-OWNERSHIP
      16.9. Future Blackboard Ownership GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-FUTURE-OWNERSHIP Any future Blackboard, cross-context reconciliation, knowledge graph, background synchronization, or autonomous coordination capability shall require separately approved requirements and architecture, expected under the future-vision requirement family rather than inferred from this current document.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-CURRENT-ACCEPTANCE
      16.10. Current Baseline Independence GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-CURRENT-ACCEPTANCE Acceptance of the current 50–53 Memory Management baseline shall not depend on implementation of a Blackboard, knowledge graph, background agent, or cross-path overlap resolver.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-BLACKBOARD
      17.17. AC: Blackboard Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-BLACKBOARD Inspection of the current baseline shall not identify MemoryContextPack, SuperPrompt.extras, or pending-topic state as satisfying a Blackboard protocol or shared coordination subsystem.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED
      16.1. No Current Blackboard Subsystem GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED The current Memory Management implementation shall not be described as providing a Blackboard coordinator, shared Blackboard schema, posting protocol, task queue, subscription mechanism, or conflict-resolution engine.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

      16.1. No Current Blackboard Subsystem GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED The current Memory Management implementation shall not be described as providing a Blackboard coordinator, shared Blackboard schema, posting protocol, task queue, subscription mechanism, or conflict-resolution engine.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-CURRENT-ACCEPTANCE
    16.10. Current Baseline Independence GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-CURRENT-ACCEPTANCE Acceptance of the current 50–53 Memory Management baseline shall not depend on implementation of a Blackboard, knowledge graph, background agent, or cross-path overlap resolver.

    17. 17. Traceability and Acceptance Criteria
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP
    17.1. Memory Merge Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemorySentenceReducer, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate, MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder, MemoryCompressor, MemoryMergeSynthesizer, and the associated memory agent JSON definitions shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNC
      17.19. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNC The 53 baseline shall not be accepted when recording position, reduction behavior, gate route, pending-state lifecycle, contributor trace, synthesis evidence classes, output schema, persistence boundary, or Blackboard status contradicts this document.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
      1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
      1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
      2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
      3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
      4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
      5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
      6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
        2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SIBLING-BOUNDARY
    17.2. Sibling Ownership Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-SIBLING-BOUNDARY MemoryRecord and file persistence shall remain owned by 51; vector ingestion, candidate retrieval, scoring, and MemoryContextPack construction shall remain owned by 52.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT
      7.1. Initial Brief Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-INIT If no previous clean ActiveBrief exists, the builder shall invoke the configured init agent using reduced current question, reduced current answer, current record metadata, and required-output text.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
        3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT
    17.3. AC: Initial ActiveBrief GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-INIT Given a first captured record with reducible Q/A and a successful init agent response, the new record shall contain the returned title/body and contributor list containing only its record_id before .ragmem append.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE
      7.5. Related ActiveBrief Update Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-NORMAL-UPDATE For gate route activebrief_update, the builder shall invoke the update agent with the previous brief and current reduced Q/A.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
        3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED
    17.4. AC: Related ActiveBrief Update GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RELATED Given a previous clean brief and current Q/A whose ActiveBrief top-score mean meets threshold, the update agent shall run, contributor IDs shall include the current record, and pending state shall clear.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP
      7.13. Unrelated Q/A Skip Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-SKIP For a non-update gate route, the builder shall skip the ActiveBrief LLM call, copy the previous ActiveBrief title/body/contributor IDs into the new record result, and create a new pending-topic buffer from the current reduced Q/A.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
        3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
