Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP
    17.5. AC: Unrelated Q/A Skip GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SKIP Given current Q/A that fails ActiveBrief and pending-topic thresholds, no ActiveBrief LLM call shall run, the previous brief shall be copied, and the current Q/A shall replace pending-topic state.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT
      7.9. Confirmed Topic-Shift Route GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEROUTE-TOPIC-SHIFT For gate route pending_topic_shift, the builder shall combine pending reduced Q/A and current reduced Q/A and shall invoke the configured update agent with the previous ActiveBrief plus that combined reduced content.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
        3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;

          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and

    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC
    17.6. AC: Confirmed Topic Shift GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPIC Given a pending skipped Q/A and a current Q/A meeting pending_topic_threshold while failing the old ActiveBrief threshold, the update agent payload shall include both labeled Q/A pairs and contributors shall include pending and current record IDs.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR
      5.4. Reject Brief with Black Contributor GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-BLACK-CONTRIBUTOR The builder shall reject a previous ActiveBrief when any listed contributor ID belongs to a previous record currently tagged Black.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN
        5.1. Newest-to-Oldest Previous Brief Scan GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-PREVIOUS-SCAN The builder shall scan previous records from newest to oldest and shall select the first eligible non-empty ActiveBrief.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
          3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
          • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
            1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
            2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
            3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
            4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
            5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
            6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK
    17.7. AC: Black Contributor Exclusion GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-BLACK Given a previous brief whose contributor is currently Black, the builder shall skip that brief when selecting previous clean continuity state.
    • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER
      4.15. Original Sentence Order Restoration GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-ORDER The reducer shall reconstruct reduced text from selected sentence indexes in their original source order rather than relevance-rank order.
      • GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT
        4.1. Reducer Input Contract GHOST-MEM-REDUCER-INPUT MemorySentenceReducer shall accept input_text and output_text and shall return ReducedQA containing reduced_question, reduced_answer, question_vectors, answer_vectors, and diagnostics.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER
    17.8. AC: Reducer Order and Budget GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-REDUCER Given over-budget Q/A with multiple windows, selected text shall remain within allocated budgets, redundant windows shall be excluded at threshold, and reconstructed sentences shall follow source order.
    • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE
      6.10. Top-Score Selection Above Twenty GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-TOP-SELECTION-LARGE For more than twenty Q/A scores, the gate shall use floor(n divided by five), with at least one score, in its top-score mean.
      • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT
        6.1. ActiveBrief Relevance Gate GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEGATE-ROOT When enabled and a previous clean ActiveBrief exists, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate shall evaluate reduced current Q/A vectors against the previous ActiveBrief and optional pending-topic center before an ActiveBrief update agent is called.
        • GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION
          3.1. ActiveRetrievalBrief Definition GHOST-MEM-ACTIVEBRIEF-DEFINITION ActiveRetrievalBrief shall be a compact, query-independent representation of the current working-conversation center for one memory history.
          • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
            1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
            2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
            3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
            4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
            5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
            6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
            • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
              2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT
    17.9. AC: Gate Top-Score Counts GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-TOPCOUNT Gate tests at n equal to 4, 5, 20, 21, and 25 shall use 4, 4, 4, 4, and 5 top scores respectively.
    • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL
      10.12. Original Episodic Text Preservation GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-PRESERVE-ORIGINAL Compressed candidates shall retain original input_text and output_text and shall add compressed_input_text and compressed_output_text as runtime fields.
      • GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT
        10.1. Runtime Memory Compressor GHOST-MEM-COMPRESSOR-ROOT MemoryCompressor shall receive runtime configuration and shall produce query-dependent compressed fields without modifying durable MemoryRecord truth.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS
    17.10. AC: Query-Anchored Episodic Compression GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-COMPRESS Given enabled compression, a non-empty query anchor, and selected episodic candidates, each result shall preserve original Q/A and add query_anchor_episode compressed fields and diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES
      11.7. Current Synthesized Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-CURRENT-EVIDENCE-CLASSES The current synthesizer evidence source shall include reduced episodic episodes and semantic memory chunks.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
        11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
          4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
          5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
          6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
          • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
            2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT
    17.11. AC: Current Synthesis Evidence Classes GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNTH-INPUT A synthesis payload shall include bounded episodic and semantic evidence but shall not include working-memory or Direct Recall candidates in memory_evidence under the current implementation.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE
      11.13. No Evidence Skip GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-NO-EVIDENCE When the formatted episodic and semantic evidence text is empty, the synthesizer shall skip the LLM and return empty memory_context with reason no_memory_evidence.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
        11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
        • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
          1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
          1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
          2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
          3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE
    17.12. AC: Empty Evidence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-EVIDENCE With no formatted episodic or semantic evidence, no synthesis LLM call shall run and returned memory_context shall be empty with no_memory_evidence diagnostics.
    • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE
      12.14. Synthesis Failure Result GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-FAILURE If composition, provider call, or parse raises an exception, the synthesizer shall return empty memory_context, a synthesis_failed diagnostic containing the error and input counts, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
      • GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID
        12.1. Memory Synthesizer Agent Identity GHOST-MEM-SYNTHAGENT-ID The current synthesizer shall resolve agent_id memory_synthesizer and agent version memory_synthesizer_001.
        • GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT
          11.1. Memory Context Synthesizer GHOST-MEM-SYNTH-ROOT MemoryMergeSynthesizer shall synthesize one query-relevant memory_context string from current episodic candidates and semantic memory chunks using the effective retrieval query and ActiveRetrievalBrief as an orientation and anti-duplication reference.
          • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
            1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
            1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
            2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
            3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
            4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
            5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
            6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.

Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE
    17.13. AC: Synthesis Failure GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-FAILURE When the Memory Context provider call or parse fails, the result shall contain empty memory_context, synthesis_failed diagnostics, and memory_synthesis_llm_skipped true.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT
      13.2. SuperPrompt Memory Context Text GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-SP-TEXT MemoryRetriever shall assign MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context to SuperPrompt.memory_context_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;

    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF
    17.14. AC: SuperPrompt Memory Handoff GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-HANDOFF After successful memory synthesis, SuperPrompt.memory_context_text shall equal MemoryContextPack.synthesized_memory_context while document stage artifacts remain unchanged.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION
      13.8. Memory Context Permission Dependency GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-PERMISSION Memory synthesis and final projection shall honor PreProcessing allow_memory_context and memory_context_policy; a request with memory context disabled shall not receive synthesized memory_context_text.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-PERMISSION
    17.15. AC: Memory Permission GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-PERMISSION When PreProcessing disables memory context, no synthesized memory context shall be rendered in the final SuperPrompt for that request.
    • GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST
      13.11. No Runtime Context Persistence GHOST-MEM-HANDOFF-NO-PERSIST memory_context_text, synthesis diagnostics, compressed episodic fields, and MemoryContextPack shall remain run-local artifacts and shall not be appended to .ragmem as new durable truth.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RUNTIME-ONLY
    17.16. AC: Runtime Artifact Non-Persistence GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-RUNTIME-ONLY After a retrieval run, compressed candidate fields and synthesized memory_context shall not appear as rewritten content in existing .ragmem records.
    • GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED
      16.1. No Current Blackboard Subsystem GHOST-MEM-BLACKBOARD-NOT-IMPLEMENTED The current Memory Management implementation shall not be described as providing a Blackboard coordinator, shared Blackboard schema, posting protocol, task queue, subscription mechanism, or conflict-resolution engine.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-BLACKBOARD
    17.17. AC: Blackboard Boundary GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-NO-BLACKBOARD Inspection of the current baseline shall not identify MemoryContextPack, SuperPrompt.extras, or pending-topic state as satisfying a Blackboard protocol or shared coordination subsystem.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE
      14.9. No Hidden Configuration Override GHOST-MEM-MERGECFG-NO-HIDDEN-OVERRIDE A Python fallback value shall not be represented as JSON-controlled when the active runtime does not read the corresponding JSON field.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-CONFIG
    17.18. AC: Configuration Consumption Truth GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-CONFIG Changing a consumed memory transformation setting shall be observable in reduction, route, cap, or diagnostics; an unconsumed or contradictory field shall not be represented as effective without code evidence.
    • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP
      17.1. Memory Merge Code Ownership GHOST-MEM-MERGE-CODE-OWNERSHIP MemorySentenceReducer, MemoryActiveBriefRelevanceGate, MemoryActiveRetrievalBriefBuilder, MemoryCompressor, MemoryMergeSynthesizer, and the associated memory agent JSON definitions shall map to the deepest applicable UIDs in this document.
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT
        1.1. Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT GHOST shall provide current memory-continuity and memory-synthesis functions that:
        1. build or propagate an ActiveRetrievalBrief during MemoryRecord capture;
        2. reduce Q/A text deterministically for continuity decisions;
        3. gate ActiveBrief updates using embedding similarity and a RAM-only pending-topic buffer;
        4. compress selected episodic memory against the effective retrieval query;
        5. synthesize episodic and semantic memory evidence into one runtime memory_context string; and
        6. expose an explicit boundary toward, but not implement, a future Blackboard capability.
        • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
          2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNC
    17.19. AC: Requirement-Code Synchronization GHOST-MEM-MERGE-AC-SYNC The 53 baseline shall not be accepted when recording position, reduction behavior, gate route, pending-state lifecycle, contributor trace, synthesis evidence classes, output schema, persistence boundary, or Blackboard status contradicts this document.
